entities.name: vivakṣā
entities.cite: sato 'vivakṣā — a real feature of the situation may be *unintended*, present but not meant

entities.name: pārārthya
entities.cite: an element may stand wholly in service of another, its sense exhausted in subordination

entities.name: linga
entities.cite: a meaning may be delivered only through its linga, its indicatory mark, never said outright

entities.name: artha
entities.cite: a meaning may be delivered only through its linga

entities.name: tarka
entities.cite: tarkena pravibhajyate, reasoning is what articulates and applies them

entities.name: nyāya
entities.cite: Iti nyayo bahuvidhah: and such maxims are many

entities.name: rūpa
entities.cite: The verse is a window onto the actual exegetical culture; form alone cannot tell you what was not-intended, what is subordinate, what is marked-at

axioms.name: vivaksite
axioms.cite: a real feature of the situation may be *unintended*, present but not meant

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: an element may stand wholly in service of another, its sense exhausted in subordination

axioms.name: vyanjaka
axioms.cite: a meaning may be delivered only through its linga, its indicatory mark, never said outright

axioms.name: bahuvidha
axioms.cite: Iti nyayo bahuvidhah: and such maxims are many

axioms.name: vibhajyate
axioms.cite: tarkena pravibhajyate, reasoning is what articulates and applies them

denials.name: samarpita
denials.cite: form alone cannot tell you what was not-intended, what is subordinate, what is marked-at — judgement must

claims.name: vyanjaka
claims.cite: indication of a meaning through a mark

rejected_readings.label: form_complete_meaning
rejected_readings.rendering: Form alone exhaustively determines meaning through intention, subordination, and marking.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts that form can directly establish artha without judgment, contradicting the verse's core point that gap between form and meaning requires tarka (reasoning) to bridge it. Denies the necessity of interpretive principles.

rejected_readings.label: reversed_linga
rejected_readings.rendering: The meaning through its mark delivers an indicatory mark, reversing the relation.
rejected_readings.why: Reverses the direction: linga does not derive from artha but rather artha is only reached through linga. This inverts the foundational hermeneutical relation the verse establishes.

rejected_readings.label: tarka_unnecessary
rejected_readings.rendering: The interpretive principles are self-evident and require no reasoning to apply.
rejected_readings.why: Denies that tarka (reasoning/judgment) articulates and applies the nyaya (maxims), contradicting the explicit statement that tarkena pravibhajyate — reasoning articulates them. Makes tarka superfluous.

notes: The verse is compressed and technical; the three canons (vivakṣā, pārārthya, vyakti laiṅgikī) are exegetical jurisprudence from Mimamsa, and the contract encodes them as named hermeneutical principles. The key claim is that tarka (reasoning/judgment) is the instrument that bridges the gap between form and meaning by applying these canons.
